title: MVVM
objectives: Bind View to ViewModel via observable state
Keep the View 'dumb' and the Model 'pure'

sections.markdown: MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) is a UI pattern where the **View** observes an immutable state exposed by the **ViewModel**, which orchestrates the **Model** (data + business rules). Perfect fit for Flutter's declarative UI.

rows: UI state + user actions; no Flutter imports ideally
Widgets that render state and call VM methods

sections.markdown: MVVM works because it enforces **one-way data flow**: state flows View ← ViewModel, actions flow View → ViewModel. Riverpod NotifierProviders, BLoC/Cubit, and ChangeNotifier are all ways to implement the ViewModel in Flutter.
Putting business logic (validation, network, mapping) inside widgets — that's a Massive-View anti-pattern. Widgets should read state and call VM methods; nothing more.

flashcards.back: Model (data + rules), ViewModel (state + actions), View (renders state, dispatches actions).
State flows View ← VM; actions flow View → VM. Strictly one-way rendering.

flashcards.front: How is MVVM implemented in Flutter?
flashcards.back: With ChangeNotifier, ValueNotifier, Riverpod Notifiers, or BLoC/Cubit exposing state.

options: Only render state and dispatch actions
quiz.explain: Widgets stay dumb; the ViewModel owns behavior.
State flows down from VM; actions flow up from View.

quiz.q: Which is NOT a common ViewModel in Flutter?
quiz.explain: The others expose observable UI state; a Cursor is a data-layer primitive.

challenges.text: Extract a widget's business logic into a ChangeNotifier VM.
Model a screen's UI as an immutable state class (loading/data/error).
Unit-test the ViewModel with a fake Model without any Flutter imports.
Migrate a Flutter screen from setState soup to a testable MVVM with Riverpod.

interviewQuestions: How do you test a ViewModel?
